hypotheses.label: Calibrated energy concentrates havoc on compact/fast/stable seeds, so its corpus assembles a LARGER / more-structured (more-token) input than naive's flat budget ever funds

measurement.registry_tool_note: REUSE the struct_size_and_token_count compute of joint_necessity, but the comparison is the 2-set calibrated-RESOLVING vs naive-BLOCKING seed sets (NOT the 3-way vpc/cmplog/vp). Orchestrator parameterizes joint_necessity to a calibrated-vs-naive seed-set diff; metrics size_lift / tag_lift carry over, vp_tags/cmp_tags/vpc_tags are unused here.

hypotheses.prediction: G1: the calibrated-RESOLVING seed set is materially larger AND carries more structural tokens than the naive-BLOCKING set (size_lift >= 1.3 AND tag_lift >= 1.3). This DISCRIMINATES from the rare-value alternative, which predicts the two sets are SAME size with the win being a single byte/codepoint flip (size_lift ~ 1.0, tag_lift ~ 1.0). It is falsifiable: if naive's blocking corpus is just as large/structured (size_lift ~ 1.0) then the win is NOT structural assembly and the hypothesis fails.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Independent of the resolve pattern: the resolve label is calibrated-resolves/naive-blocks; this measures the STATIC structural content of each fuzzer's saved seed set, not whether either resolves. A larger structured corpus on the calibrated side is the cause, observed orthogonally to the resolve outcome.

non_decidable_members.reason: libxml2 has NO on-disk corpus (data_realities) -> seed_set descriptor cannot score it; falls to inconclusive.
libxml2 has NO on-disk corpus -> seed_set descriptor cannot score it; falls to inconclusive.

hypotheses.label: Calibrated energy raises per-seed havoc count on near-valid seeds, raising the probability of landing a specific RARE byte / multi-byte codepoint value at the gating position; the win is value-precision, NOT a larger structure

measurement.new_descriptor_note: UNBUILT. Proposed: at the gate offset, measure how CLOSE each fuzzer's seed set gets to the gate's required literal/character-class value (min Hamming/byte distance to {0x2D,0x2E,0xB7} for libxml2_7126; min distance of the 4-byte follower codepoint to {0x112E0,0x112E5,0x112E6,0x112E7,0x112E8} for harfbuzz_6114; presence of literal 0x3c '<' in a parseable SELECT for sqlite3_14451). Metric value_dist_lift = (naive min-distance) / (calibrated min-distance) at the gate position; calibrated reaching the value (or closer) => lift > 1.

hypotheses.prediction: G1: the calibrated-RESOLVING seed set REACHES (or comes closer to) the gate literal at the gate position than naive's blocking set (value_dist_lift > 1 OR calibrated_hits_value == true while naive does not), WITHOUT a corresponding structural-size increase (this is what separates it from structural_assembly, which predicts a size/token lift). Falsifiable: if naive's seeds are equally close to the literal, the value-precision story fails.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Independent of the resolve pattern: measures byte-level proximity to a FIXED gate literal in each fuzzer's static seed set, not the resolve outcome. Valid because these gates are single-offset VALUE checks with same-sized W/L seeds (byte alignment holds, unlike the assembly subtype).

non_decidable_members.reason: libxml2 has NO on-disk corpus (data_realities) -> seed_set descriptor cannot score it; falls to inconclusive.

DECIDABLE in principle (sqlite3 corpus exists) BUT the gate literal '<' is a parser-grammar token, not a fixed-offset byte; value_distance_reached must check PRESENCE-in-parseable-context, which the unbuilt tool must support. Marked decidable=partial pending that capability.

hypotheses.scheduling_caveat: The calibrated-vs-naive delta is PURELY a power-schedule (energy allocation) effect with identical mutator vocab and identical coverage feedback. For value-precision gates the only mechanistic difference is per-seed TRIAL COUNT, not corpus admission. A static seed-set snapshot may show naive eventually accumulating seeds equally close to the literal (just slower), so value_dist_lift can read ~1.0 even when the live advantage was real. Where the snapshot cannot separate calibrated from naive on value distance, the member is NON-DISCRIMINABLE -> decidable:false -> inconclusive (honest).

merges_and_collapses.kept_separate: structural_assembly (size+token lift) and rare_value_targeting (value-distance, NO size lift) ARE discriminable by a real measurement: assembly predicts size_lift>=1.3, rare-value predicts size_lift~1.0 with a value-proximity gain. Kept as two hypotheses.
merges_and_collapses.no_further_merge: Within structural_assembly the per-target substrate differs (DTD depth vs font tables vs SQL expression depth) but the MEASUREMENT is the same struct_size_and_token_count diff with target-specific --tokens, so they collapse into ONE hypothesis (no measurement separates them, per job 2).
merges_and_collapses.discarded: No independent/anti split: the shape is uniformly calibrated-pro (all covers_pairs = 'minimizer>naive (calibrated_energy)').

notes: TARGETS: members span libxml2(3), curl(1), harfbuzz(6), sqlite3(3). UNBUILT TOOLS: (a) joint_necessity REUSED but must be re-parameterized to a 2-set calibrated-vs-naive seed-set diff (currently does a 3-way vpc/cmplog/vp diff) — straightforward; (b) value_distance_reached at source=seed_set is NEW (registry_tool:null) and must be built, incl. a parseable-context presence check for sqlite3's grammar-token gate. NON-DISCRIMINABLE / inconclusive: all 3 libxml2 members (6414, 6690, 7126) have NO on-disk corpus (data_realities: corpora only for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3) so NEITHER seed_set descriptor can score them -> inconclusive. SCHEDULING CAVEAT (load-bearing honesty): calibrated-vs-naive is a pure power-schedule difference (same mutators, same feedback, same admission) — the win is per-seed energy, not what the corpus is ALLOWED to admit. A static seed-set snapshot may therefore fail to separate calibrated from naive (naive reaches similar seeds, just slower), especially for the rare-value subtype where the only delta is trial count. Members whose snapshot shows no size/value lift are honestly NON-DISCRIMINABLE and route to fallback:inconclusive rather than being force-labeled. DECIDABLE coverage: structural_assembly fully decidable on curl(1)+harfbuzz(5)+sqlite3(2)=8 members; rare_value decidable on harfbuzz(1)+sqlite3(1, partial)=2 members; 3 libxml2 members inconclusive by data_realities.
